# polaris_rest_call

`polaris_rest_call` runs a Polaris REST call during Terraform refresh. It is useful for discovery, validation, and output values when Terraform should not own the lifecycle of the remote object.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "polaris_rest_call" "catalogs" {
  provider = polaris.management

  operation_id = "listCatalogs"
}

output "catalogs_json" {
  value     = data.polaris_rest_call.catalogs.response_body
  sensitive = true
}
```

## Schema

### Optional

- `operation_id` (String) OpenAPI operation ID from the generated Polaris operation registry.
- `method` (String) HTTP method fallback when `operation_id` is not set.
- `path` (String) HTTP path fallback when `operation_id` is not set.
- `path_params` (Map of String) Values for OpenAPI path placeholders.
- `query_params` (Map of String) Query string parameters.
- `headers` (Map of String) Additional HTTP headers.
- `body` (String, Sensitive) Optional JSON request body for advanced read-style operations.
- `expected_status_codes` (List of Number) Accepted status codes. Defaults to `200`.

### Read-Only

- `status_code` (Number) HTTP status code.
- `response_body` (String, Sensitive) JSON response body.
